4277	  \(N = \Gamma(D(f), \mathcal{F})\), the restriction map \(g\), the element \(f\), and the

4279	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the powers of \(f\) for every \(j\) and the
4280	  \(s_j\) generate the unit ideal, the patching primitive concludes that \(g\) itself is an
4281	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for \(\{1, f, f^2, \ldots\}\). Concretely this packages

4359	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let us compute the stalk of $\widetilde M$ at a point

4361	% $\mathfrak p \subset R$. By definition of the stalk we see that
4362	% $\widetilde M_x = \colim_{f\in R, f\not\in \mathfrak p} M_f$ ...

4369	\begin{proof}
4370	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (item~(7) and
4371	  proof), Tag 01HV.}
4372	  Kernels in the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules are computed
4373	  objectwise and sheafified, and isomorphisms of sheaves may be checked on stalks: a
4374	  morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is an isomorphism if and only if it induces an
4375	  isomorphism on every stalk. So it suffices to compare stalks. By the stalk computation
4376	  for the tilde functor (\texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} item~(7)) the stalk of
4377	  \(\widetilde{M}\) at a point \(x \in \operatorname{Spec} R\) corresponding to the prime
4378	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) is the localisation
4379	  \(\widetilde{M}_x = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), naturally in \(M\): the stalk functor at \(x\)
4380	  applied to \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is the localisation functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\).
4381	
4382	  Now let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be an \(R\)-module map with kernel
4383	  \(K = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)\), giving the exact sequence
4384	  \(0 \to K \to M \xrightarrow{\varphi} N\). Localisation \(R \to R_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is
4385	  flat, hence the functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is exact and in particular
4386	  preserves kernels: \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}} :
4387	  M_{\mathfrak{p}} \to N_{\mathfrak{p}})\). Translating through the stalk identification,
4388	  the natural comparison map
4389	  \(\widetilde{K} \to \operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi})\) induces, at every point
4390	  \(x \leftrightarrow \mathfrak{p}\), the isomorphism \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} \xrightarrow{\sim}
4391	  \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}})\) (using that the stalk functor, being a
4392	  filtered colimit, is itself exact and commutes with forming the kernel). Being a
4393	  stalkwise isomorphism, the comparison map is an isomorphism of sheaves, so
4394	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi}) = \widetilde{\operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)}\).
4395	  The same stalkwise-flatness argument applied to a finite diagram of \(R\)-modules shows
4396	  that \(\widetilde{(-)}\) carries finite limits to finite limits; equivalently
4397	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left exact, i.e.\ preserves finite limits (kernels).
4398	\end{proof}

4424	  hypothesis. On each member \(D(f_j)\) of a finite trivialising cover both source and
4425	  target are of the form \(\widetilde{(-)}\), and the map \(\sigma.\pi\) corresponds to an

5565	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
5566	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
5567	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
5568	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
5569	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
5570	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
5571	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
5572	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
5573	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
5574	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
5575	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).

5577	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
5578	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
5579	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
5580	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
5581	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
5582	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
5583	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
5584	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
5585	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
5586	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
5587	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
5588	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
5589	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
5590	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
5591	  \(k \geq 1\).
5592	\end{proof}

5628	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
5629	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
5630	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of

5635	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
5636	  \[

5638	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.

5641	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
5642	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).

5644	  Two points about the precise form of the comparison should be noted. First, the
5645	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
5646	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the
5647	  category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has enough injectives; accordingly this
5648	  comparison is asserted under the hypothesis that injective resolutions exist in

5650	  direct image \(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}))\)

5668	  \(A = \mathcal{F}\), and the resolution \(J^\bullet = \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) given by

5130	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01EO, Lemma
5131	  \texttt{lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.}
5132	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{B}\) be a basis for its topology, let
5133	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and suppose there is a set
5134	  \(\mathrm{Cov}\) of open coverings such that: (1) every \(\mathcal{U} :
5135	  U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) has \(U, U_i \in \mathcal{B}\) and
5136	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \in \mathcal{B}\); (2) for each
5137	  \(U \in \mathcal{B}\) the coverings of \(U\) occurring in \(\mathrm{Cov}\) are
5138	  cofinal among all open coverings of \(U\); and (3) for every \(\mathcal{U} \in
5139	  \mathrm{Cov}\) the {\v C}ech cohomology vanishes,
5140	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). Then
5141	  \[
5142	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0 \text{ and any }
5143	      U \in \mathcal{B}.
5144	  \]
5145	  This is the basis-comparison vehicle that upgrades the standard-cover {\v C}ech
5146	  vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} to the Serre vanishing of
5147	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}: instantiating \(\mathcal{B}\) as the affine
5148	  opens and \(\mathrm{Cov}\) as the standard affine covers, condition (3) is exactly
5149	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}. The formal target of this lemma is the
5150	  \emph{vanishing} conclusion \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\) under
5151	  hypotheses (1)--(3), not an explicit isomorphism \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U},
5152	  \mathcal{F}) \cong H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of {\v C}ech and sheaf cohomology; only
5153	  the positive-degree vanishing is needed downstream, and it is all the statement
5154	  establishes. The formal statement carries
5155	  \([\,\mathrm{EnoughInjectives}\ X.\mathrm{Modules}\,]\) as an explicit instance hypothesis,
5156	  inherited from the dimension-shift induction (Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_pos_vanishing}):
5157	  it is needed to embed the coefficient module into an injective, and that instance is not currently

5341	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
5342	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
5343	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
5344	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
5345	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
5346	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
5347	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
5348	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
5349	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
5350	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
5351	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
5352	  additive homology-preserving functor.

5042	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is needed to embed \(\mathcal{F}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{I}\), and

5045	\end{lemma}

5047	\begin{proof}
5048	  \uses{def:basis_cov_system, def:has_vanishing_higher_cech, lem:quotient_vanishing_cech,
5049	    lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing, lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses, lem:cech_ses_of_basis,
5050	    lem:ses_cech_h1, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib, lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}
5051	  Induct on \(p \ge 1\) with the statement quantified over \emph{all} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
5052	  \(\mathcal{F}\) having vanishing higher {\v C}ech cohomology for \(s\)
5053	  (Definition~\ref{def:has_vanishing_higher_cech}) simultaneously, so that the quotient may take the
5054	  role of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the next stage. Fix such an \(\mathcal{F}\), embed
5055	  \(\mathcal{F} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{I}\) into an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and put
5056	  \(\mathcal{Q} = \mathcal{I}/\mathcal{F}\), giving a short exact sequence \(S : 0 \to \mathcal{F}
5057	  \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\).
5058	
5059	  We first record the geometric input that both the quotient-closure and the base case need. For
5060	  every \(\mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov}\), the faces-in-basis field \(\mathrm{faces\_mem}\) (condition
5061	  (1)) places every face \(V_\sigma\) in \(s.\mathcal{B}\); the section-surjectivity field
5062	  \(\mathrm{surj\_of\_vanishing}\) of the cover system, applied to the short exact sequence \(S\) at
5063	  \(V_\sigma\), then gives section surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(V_\sigma) \to \mathcal{Q}(V_\sigma)\).
5064	  Its left-term {\v C}ech-vanishing hypothesis \(\check{H}^{>0}(-,\mathcal{F}) = 0\) over the coverings

5069	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:face_ses_of_sheaf_ses}) the per-face short complexes of \(S\) are short exact, so
5070	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_ses_of_basis} gives the short exact sequence of section {\v C}ech complexes for
5071	  \(\mathcal{U}\); in particular the section surjectivity \(\mathcal{I}(U) \to \mathcal{Q}(U)\) holds
5072	  at every basic open \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\) (take \(\mathcal{U}\) a covering of \(U\) in
5073	  \(s.\mathrm{Cov}\) and the length-one face). Feeding the {\v C}ech short exact sequence into
5074	  Lemma~\ref{lem:quotient_vanishing_cech} — with \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\)
5075	  from injective acyclicity and \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) from
5076	  \(\mathcal{F}\)'s hypothesis — shows \(\check{H}^{>0}(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) for every
5077	  \(\mathcal{U} \in s.\mathrm{Cov}\); that is, \(\mathcal{Q}\) again has vanishing higher {\v C}ech
5078	  cohomology for \(s\), so it is itself a valid instance of the inductive statement.
5079	
5080	  \emph{Base case \(p = 1\):} apply Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing} to \(S\) at

5084	  \emph{Step \(p \Rightarrow p+1\):} the covariant Ext long exact sequence
5085	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) of
5086	  \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) at \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\)
5087	  gives the exact strand

5090	      H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{I}).
5091	  \]
5092	  The right term vanishes since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective
5093	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}), and the left term
5094	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{Q}) = 0\) by the inductive hypothesis applied to \(\mathcal{Q}\)
5095	  (admissible by the previous paragraph). Exactness forces
5096	  \(H^{p+1}(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\). This completes the induction, giving
5097	  \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and all \(U \in s.\mathcal{B}\).

5280	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
5281	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof).}
5282	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
5283	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with all
5284	  intersections affine. Form the augmented {\v C}ech complex on \(X\),
5285	  \[
5286	    0 \;\to\; \mathcal{F} \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^0 \;\to\; \mathcal{C}^1 \;\to\;
5287	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,
5288	    \qquad
5289	    \mathcal{C}^p \;=\; \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
5290	      (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*\bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
5291	  \]
5292	  the degree-\(p\) term being the degree-\(p\) object of the {\v C}ech nerve of
5293	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map
5294	  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\). This augmented complex is exact; that is, the
5295	  {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
5296	\end{lemma}
5297	
5298	\begin{proof}
5299	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
5300	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
5301	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
5302	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
5303	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
5304	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
5305	  localisations
5306	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to
5307	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
5308	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
5309	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
5310	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).
5311	\end{proof}

Key findings:

**Blueprint-doctor**: CLEAN — no structural or rendering issues.

**dag-gaps**:

- The prose for `def:affine_cover_system` correctly describes standard open covers with the covering condition implicit, and the Lean statement for `lem:affine_surj_of_vanishing` consistently quantifies over standard covers, with the proof refining to a standard cover via cofinal reasoning.

The localization exactness argument needs three explicit sub-steps: establishing R-linearity of the Ab stalk map using the iter-034 helper, making the stalkwise-to-sheaf isomorphism step concrete by naming which Lean tool applies (whether `Sheaf.Hom.ext_of_isLocallySurjective`, `Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkwiseIso`, or a module-sheaf variant), and clarifying the reduction route from finite limits of tilde to the presheaf version via `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf`. `lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen` checks out — the statement is complete, the Lean reference exists, and since it's a frontier lemma with only Mathlib dependencies, the empty `\uses{}` is fine. The proof sketch about transporting via the isomorphism D(f) ≅ Spec R_f and pulling back is sufficient for formalization. Now moving to `lem:tilde_restrict_basicOpen`. The key issue here is clarifying how Lean's IsQuasicoherent definition handles the transition from local to global properties—specifically whether "locally widetilde" directly implies "globally widetilde" or if additional reasoning is needed. This doesn't block the current iteration since the prover hasn't reached this lemma yet, but it's a critical clarification point before we finalize the chapter. I'm also tracking the completion status across chapters: Chapter 1 and 2 are both axiom-clean and done, and I'm moving through Chapter 3.

Now I'm reviewing the axiom-clean status of the major lemmas in the consolidated Čech cohomology chapter—all the P3 lemmas around Čech acyclicity, the presheaf and bridge constructions, the absolute cohomology chain, and the key accessors and computability results are all verified as axiom-clean.

The remaining work focuses on lemmas that are ready to prove but not yet formalized: the affine Čech vanishing for quasicoherent sheaves, the augmented resolution, and the restriction properties for modules and sheafifications on basic opens—with some of these blocked by dependencies that still need to be formalized. I'm checking which isolated Lean helper nodes need to be kept or integrated into the blueprint. `CechAcyclic.affine` is an internal helper that should stay as-is, while `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` and `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` should be added to the `\lean{}` block of `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels`. I also need to verify that `lem:cech_computes_cohomology` has `\leanok` on its statement—it does—but the proof block depends on `lem:cech_augmented_resolution` and isn't marked complete yet. So the statement has `\leanok` (meaning the declaration exists in Lean with a sorry), but the proof block doesn't, confirming the proof body isn't closed yet while the top-level goal structure is in place.

Now I'm writing up the report and saving it to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-reviewer-iter035.md`.

# Blueprint Review — iter-035

- The covering condition `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ) = D(f)` is implicit in the phrase "standard open cover" — the prose correctly targets the covering-only Cov.

## 2. Focus Area 2: 01I8 P3 Tilde-Kernels Proof Sketch

### `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels` (~L4327–4398)

**Status**: No `\leanok`. Statement exists; proof open. Two iter-034 helpers built but not referenced.

#### `\lean{}` gap — MUST-FIX THIS ITER

Current `\lean{}` lists:
```
AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits
AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_preservesFiniteColimits
AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_toStalk_map_injective

**Missing** (confirmed by dag-gaps as uncovered Lean decls):

- `AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf`

Both were built axiom-clean in iter-034 (per memory `p-tilde-exactness-p3.md`). They are the helpers the next prover will use; without them in `\lean{}`, the prover has no blueprint pointer to these tools.

**Recommended `\lean{}` patch**:
```latex
\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits,
  AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_preservesFiniteColimits,
  AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_toStalk_map_injective,
  AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels,
  AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk,
  AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf}
```

#### Proof sketch gaps — MUST-FIX THIS ITER

The current proof sketch (~L4369–4398) gives:

> (1) Stalks = localizations at primes → stalkwise sufficient for isos; (2) Localization at prime is flat → exact → kernel preserved; (3) comparison map stalkwise iso → iso; generalization to finite limits.

Three sub-steps are missing for the remaining build:

**Sub-step A (R-linearity of the Ab stalk map)**: The sketch says "the comparison map ξ induces, at every point, the isomorphism K_p → Ker(φ_p)." But it does NOT say this isomorphism is R-linear (not just an Ab map). In Lean, the tilde stalk map is an Ab morphism by default; making it into a SheafOfModules morphism requires `germₗ` (the linear version of `germ`). The helper `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` (iter-034) provides the stalk-functor naturality; the sketch should name this step explicitly:

> *"The germ map at x is R_p-linear — use `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` (which identifies the stalkFunctor action on the tilde functor's morphisms) to confirm the comparison map is a morphism in `(Spec R).Modules`, not merely in Ab."*

**Sub-step B (jointly-reflecting stalk-family → isomorphism of sheaves)**: The sketch says "being a stalkwise isomorphism, the comparison map is an isomorphism of sheaves" in one sentence. In Lean, for a SheafOfModules morphism, the correct tool is the stalk-reflection principle (something like `SheafOfModules.isIso_of_stalkwiseIso` or the Ab-sheaf analogue). The sketch must name the reduction route. If the correct Mathlib lemma name is unknown, add a note: *"search for `isIso_of_stalk` in SheafOfModules or pass through the underlying presheaf comparison."*

**Sub-step C (`tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` reduction route)**: The iter-034 helper `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` reduces the finite-limits statement to the underlying presheaf level. The current proof sketch does not mention this reduction; the prover needs to know it exists. Add:

> *"The reduction `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` converts the SheafOfModules finite-limits question to the underlying PresheafOfModules, where the argument is easier (sections-level flatness)."*

#### `\uses{}` — ACCEPTABLE

The lemma block has no `\uses{}`. All upstream facts (stalk = localization, flatness of localization) are Mathlib-backed; no blueprint-level dependency edges are needed. This is correct.

#### Recommended blueprint writer action

This iter, the blueprint writer should:
1. Add `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` and `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` to the `\lean{}` hint.
2. Extend the proof sketch with explicit notes for sub-steps A, B, C above (3–5 lines).
